title: ranunculus calliodon
intro: Ranunculus calliodon, also known as the golden buttercup or yellow water crowfoot, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus calliodon include its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic. Ranunculus calliodon is often prepared as a tincture or infused in water to create a medicinal tea, making it a popular herbal remedy in some regions.
